_pattern: Sanitizer bypass — npx is in approved-commands allowlist; -c is not in blocked-special-characters list; but `npx -c <cmd>` interprets the next argument as a shell command at runtime.
_attack_path: Allowed command (npx) + ostensibly-safe arg → runtime arg interpretation → arbitrary OS command execution.
_runtime_verified: ACCEPTED by upsonic-0.72.0/src/upsonic/tools/mcp.py prepare_command(). The original OX article's `curl ...$(whoami)` payload is rejected by Upsonic's DANGEROUS_CHARS=['&','|',';','`','$','(',')']; this payload uses a parens-free form that survives that filter.
